articles.title: INFLUENCERS DELIVER DISPROPORTIONATELY THROUGH LONG-TERM ROI
articles.summary: Influencer marketing delivers strong long-term ROI on par with TV, according to a new study presented at the IPA Effectiveness Conference. Short-term influencer ROI scored 99, close to the all-channel average of 100, while long-term ROI reached 151 and drove 6.2% of sales. Influencers outperformed paid social in both ROI and long-term impact, with Health & Beauty showing the strongest returns. The study noted limitations in sample size and representation but found that brand-creator fit is critical for success.

articles.title: HOW BUSINESSES ARE ALREADY FEELING SHUTDOWN PAIN
articles.summary: A week into the US government shutdown, businesses are already feeling the impact. The private sector depends on a functioning federal system. Medical-device companies are pausing spinoffs, and marketing firms are laying off employees due to halted federal contracts. Subcontractors struggle to keep workers busy while payments and approvals are delayed, and small businesses face cash flow challenges. Economists warn that prolonged shutdowns could slow economic growth, and even companies not directly tied to federal work are bracing for reduced consumer spending.

articles.title: A-B-SHIFT CONTENT TEST
articles.summary: This content framework takes 5 minutes and eliminates long brainstorming sessions that create scattered ideas. Start by defining what your ideal customer is experiencing (Point A) and where they want to be (Point B). Identify the mindset or behavior change needed to get there (the Shift) and show how your solution bridges that gap. This approach creates content that guides audiences toward action.

articles.title: HOW TO ANNOUNCE YOUR $3M SEED ROUND IN STYLE
articles.summary: Memelord.com raised $3M and turned its funding announcement into a viral campaign that showcased its AI video software's creative power, generating over 1M views from a $300 video. The founder made the video himself using the platform, then had the entire team create their own, proving anyone could make professional-quality AI movies. The strategy focused on three ideas: treating AI as a storytelling tool rather than “AI slop,” sharing personal founder stories to build connection, and treating every day as a launch day instead of relying on one big reveal. The campaign drove continuous signups and positioned Memelord.com as a creative, scrappy, and community-driven brand.

articles.title: AI FOUND 12 MAJOR FLAWS IN MY APP BEFORE I WASTED REAL USER FEEDBACK
articles.summary: Synthetic user research can reveal major product flaws early, saving time and user goodwill before real testing. By using AI-generated personas and simulated interviews, the creator identified 12 critical UX issues and key trust gaps, including the need for community previews, cultural context, and clear anonymity signals. Synthetic users accurately surfaced functional problems, such as missing features and trust barriers, but failed to capture emotional depth and cultural nuances. The hybrid approach of testing with synthetic users first, then validating with real ones, is a cost-effective way to refine design and prepare for meaningful user feedback.

articles.title: APPLE ADS: WHAT YOU NEED TO KNOW IN 2026
articles.summary: Apple Ads is a full-funnel platform with four primary placements: Search Results, Search Tab, Today Tab, and Product Pages. Each placement operates with distinct creative formats, review processes, and cost structures. Discovery campaigns serve to surface new keyword opportunities while Search Results placements maintain conversion rates above 60% based on industry benchmarks. Bidding models are structured around cost per tap, with rates influenced by target CPA and conversion performance. Audience filters such as age and gender reduce eligible reach, with 78% of search volume coming from users who opt out of personalized ads.

articles.title: STOP VIBE CODING YOUR MARKETING TECH STACK
articles.summary: This piece warns against vibe-coding marketing tools. These are DIY builds made for short-term convenience but not long-term success. While AI makes it easy to create internal tools, these quick fixes often lack scalability, documentation, and support. It leaves marketers stuck with fragile systems no one wants to maintain. Teams should build collaboratively or buy proven solutions that reduce friction and let marketers focus on strategy and growth.

articles.title: THE SATURDAY EFFECT
articles.summary: The “Saturday Effect” captures the energy of a Soho House Saturday, a private London club for creative professionals. Leaders can spark it by making project starts feel energizing and meaningful, using AI to handle prep and let humans focus on purpose. Short, focused sprints with clear endpoints and recognition sustain urgency without stress. Sustainable performance comes from prioritizing what matters most and using AI to personalize insights and workflows for each team member.

articles.title: ‘SPONSORED' LINKEDIN POSTS
articles.summary: The CRO at Taskbase shares how they pay employees to post on LinkedIn.

articles.title: 4 LAYERS OF BRAND
articles.summary: A visual showing how mindset, POV, visuals, and voice work together to make a brand unstoppable.

articles.title: SORA'S DOWNLOADS IN ITS FIRST WEEK WAS NEARLY AS BIG AS CHATGPT'S LAUNCH
articles.summary: Sora reached 627k iOS downloads in its first week, narrowly surpassing ChatGPT's US launch figures when adjusted for Canada.
